key_assumptions.source: Mid-cycle owner cash: FY2024 dividend $20/sh and FY2025 $17.50/sh despite loss; normalized below peak $47.83 EPS
Mid-cycle owner cash $22/sh, cotton/tomato normalization after FY2025 NRV/flood charges

open_questions: Economic NAV uses probability weights; **400k AF** still unverified in filings (see `fact_check_water_nav_2026-06-02.md`)
Obtain company or district acre-foot schedule and transfer price comps on 102k+ AF moves
Verify BWEL OTC disclosure cadence and late-filing notice resolution
Review family control and dividend policy through next crop cycle
thesis: J.G. Boswell Company is a family-controlled California agribusiness founded in 1921 and headquartered in Corcoran. **[Fact]** The company produces, processes, and markets cotton, cottonseed products, tomatoes, small grains, and orchard crops on a large scale in the San Joaquin Valley. **[Fact]** It also owns subsurface oil, gas, and mineral interests on its land and on third-party land in California and Oregon (`investor-documents/ir-bwel/2025-06-30_Annual_Report.pdf`, note 1). **[Fact]** Shares trade OTC Pink (BWEL) with very thin volume; the company is not an SEC registrant and publishes audited annual reports to shareholders via OTC Markets. **[Fact]** Fiscal year ends June 30; FY2025 had 964,210 shares outstanding and stockholders' equity of about $755 million (~$783 per share).
why_market_wrong: The stock may look expensive on trailing earnings after a loss year while hiding a durable low-cost farm franchise and balance-sheet optionality. **[Inference]** FY2025 net loss reflects inventory net-realizable-value charges, flood-related costs, and crop volatility, not a permanent collapse of earning power (FY2024 net income was $46.7 million). **[Inference]** At roughly $549 per share the market prices below GAAP book (~$783), which itself likely understates land, water, and mineral rights carried at historical cost ($106 million land line). **[Inference]** OTC illiquidity and Boswell family control keep the name off institutional radars, so the discount may persist without a catalyst.
